ops.text: E32 SCREEN lane — STRUCTURAL SURPRISE, check-back (not improvising). Ground-truth token measurement contradicts the arm design. Setup verified OK: harness venv at ~/.cc-steer/experiments/E32/.venv (cc-steer @ git+file@main, spawnllm 0.6.2, manyshot present); frozen 628 loads (335 pos/293 neg, 86 sessions); TRAIN pool 6731 rows is ALREADY fully session-disjoint from the 628 (train∩eval sessions = 0), so the disjointness filter is a verified no-op keeping all 6731. Nested seed=32 permutation, prefix K rows. PROBLEM: exemplar_demo carries draft_text() = full context-window tail capped at DRAFT_CHAR_CAP=10_000 chars (median ~5.6k, max ~10k), so each demo is ~5k chars — NOT the short steering message the pre-reg cost model assumed. Real chars/token = 2.14 (measured, code-heavy), not ~4. Prefix sizes: K=125 → 695,640 chars = 325,358 tokens (MEASURED via one real sonnet call, cache_creation=325,356, cost $1.96, reply parsed '1: 0.1'); linear/additive → K=250 ≈ 691k tok, K=500 ≈ 1.37M tok, K=1000 ≈ 2.68M tok. The K=125 call SUCCEEDED at 325k>200k, so the claude CLI is using sonnet's 1M-context beta. CONSEQUENCE: K=500 (1.37M) and K=1000 (2.68M) OVERFLOW the 1M ceiling — cannot run as literal counts. Only K∈{125,250} are context-feasible. The pre-registered KILL RULE needs 125→250→500 monotonicity (3 screen points); K=500 infeasible ⇒ can't evaluate as registered. Also the module default EXEMPLAR_BUDGET=40_000 chars fits only ~8 full demos, itself inconsistent with K=125-1000. Options put to orchestrator: (A) rescale screen to K∈{125,250} only (~$9.5, 2-point trend); (B) cap per-demo chars ~1.5k so all 4 arms fit <1M (~$12.3, but truncated-context exemplars = methodological change, needs harness-local renderer since module read-only); (C) reinterpret K as native budget_chars ladder 40k/80k/160k/320k → ~8/16/32/64 full-fidelity demos; (D) abort + re-register. Ledger spend so far: $1.96 (one token probe). No screen scoring run yet — awaiting decision.
